rawContent: {"stage":"review-fix","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"The route accepts studentId from the request body and uses it directly, bypassing the authenticated user's identity — any student can impersonate another by passing a different studentId in the body.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"A check-then-insert pattern (find duplicate then create) has a race condition: two concurrent requests can both pass the duplicate check and create two submissions.","scope":"src/app/api/assignments/[id]/submit/route.ts"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"The authenticated user identity must come from the session or token, never from client-supplied body parameters — body studentId should be ignored or validated against the auth identity.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"To prevent race-condition duplicate submissions, use a unique database constraint on (assignmentId, studentId) and handle the constraint violation error, rather than a check-then-insert pattern.","scope":"src/app/api/assign
